name: Teddy Bear Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Scald 1/2 cup milk; stir in 3 tbsp sugar, 2 tsp salt, and 3 tbsp margarine. Cool to lukewarm.
2. Dissolve 1 package Active Dry Yeast in 1 1/2 cups warm water. Add the milk mixture and 3 cups of unsifted flour; beat until smooth. Stir in enough more flour (3 1/2-4 cups) to make a stiff dough.
3. On a floured board, knead the dough for 8-10 minutes. Place in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise until doubled, about 1 hour.
4. Punch down the dough. Divide into 4 equal parts. Cut one piece in half and another piece into 14 pieces. Shape into balls.
5. Place large balls on greased baking sheets for bodies. Place medium balls above them for heads. Flatten slightly and pinch seams. Attach small balls for paws, noses, and ears.
6. Cover the doughnuts and let rise until doubled again, about 1 hour.
7. Make indentations in the ears. Mix 1 egg with 1 tbsp water and brush on the bears.
8. Bake at 400°F for 25-30 minutes. Cool.
